mission.title: Install and Configure Ingress NGINX Controller
mission.description: Ingress NGINX Controller is a Kubernetes Ingress controller that uses NGINX as a reverse proxy and load balancer. It is suitable for managing external access to services in a Kubernetes cluster. However, it is important to note that the project is retiring and will not receive further updates after March 2026.

steps.title: Add the Ingress NGINX Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Ingress NGINX Helm chart repository to your Helm client.

```bash

helm repo add ingress-nginx https://kubernetes.github.io/ingress-nginx

```

> This URL points to the official Helm chart repository.

steps.title: Install the Ingress NGINX Controller
steps.description:
This command installs the Ingress NGINX Controller in the 'ingress-nginx' namespace using the specified version.

```bash

helm install ingress-nginx ingress-nginx/ingress-nginx --namespace ingress-nginx --create-namespace --version 4.10.1

```

> Ensure the namespace is created if it doesn't exist.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Ingress NGINX pods are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n ingress-nginx

```

> You should see the Ingress NGINX controller pods in a Running state.

steps.title: Create an example Ingress resource
steps.description:
This step creates a sample Ingress resource to test the Ingress NGINX Controller.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: example-ingress
  namespace: default
spec:
  rules:
  - host: example.local
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: example-service
            port:
              number: 80
EOF

```

> Replace 'example.local' and 'example-service' with your actual values.

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: example-ingress
  namespace: default
spec:
  rules:
  - host: example.local

EOF

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Ingress NGINX Controller
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Ingress NGINX Controller and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete any Ingress resources created.

```bash

helm uninstall ingress-nginx --namespace ingress-nginx

```

> This will remove the Ingress NGINX installation.

uninstall.title: Delete the Ingress NGINX namespace
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the namespace where the Ingress NGINX Controller was installed. Ensure that you have no other resources in this namespace.

```bash

kubectl delete namespace ingress-nginx

```

> This will remove the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Ingress NGINX Controller
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your Ingress resources. This command upgrades the Ingress NGINX Controller to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get ingress -A -o yaml > ingress-backup.yaml

helm upgrade ingress-nginx ingress-nginx/ingress-nginx --namespace ingress-nginx --version 4.10.1

kubectl get pods -n ingress-nginx

```

> Backup existing Ingress resources.
> Upgrade to the specified version.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Ingress" in version "networking.k8s.io/v1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error occurs if the Kubernetes version is lower than 1.19, which does not support the networking.k8s.io/v1 API.

**Fix:**
Upgrade your Kubernetes cluster to at least version 1.19.

**Affected versions:** Kubernetes versions < 1.19

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'service "example-service" not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the service referenced in the Ingress resource does not exist.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the service is created and is in the same namespace as the Ingress resource.

troubleshooting.title: Ingress not routing traffic as expected
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if the Ingress resource is misconfigured or if the NGINX controller is not running.

**Fix:**
Check the Ingress resource configuration and ensure the NGINX controller pods are in a Running state.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create listener: address already in use'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that the port is already being used by another service.

**Fix:**
Check for other services using the same port and resolve the conflict.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for custom error pages and improved health checks.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of annotations for certain configurations.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Review and update your Ingress resources to comply with the new configuration standards.

resolution.summary: You should see the Ingress NGINX pods running with the command 'kubectl get pods -n ingress-nginx' returning a status of 'Running'.

      paths:

        backend:

            port:
              number: 80
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
